# Token Calendar

> A privacy-first calendar heatmap for AI coding token spend. It turns any usage export (CSV or JSON) into a GitHub-contributions-style heatmap so you can see daily cost, token volume, peak days, streaks, and per-model breakdown at a glance. The entire app is a single HTML file that runs fully in the browser — no backend, no tracking, no account. All data stays in the browser's localStorage.

## What it is
- A single self-contained `index.html` (vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, ~1,800 lines, zero dependencies, no build step).
- Open it by double-clicking the file, or use the hosted version. Works offline.
- Tool-agnostic: built for Claude Code / Anthropic, Codex / OpenAI, Gemini CLI, or any tool that can export usage with a date, a token count, and a cost.

## Key features
- Calendar heatmap of daily token volume or cost, color-graded.
- Day / Week / Month / Year views with totals, daily average, peak day, longest streak.
- Per-model breakdown per day.
- 9 UI languages: English, 中文, 日本語, 한국어, Español, Français, Deutsch, Português, Русский.
- Dark / light themes with selectable accent color.
- Exportable share poster (PNG) of the month, with a QR code back to the tool.
- 100% local — nothing is uploaded.

## Importing data
The importer auto-detects columns from a header row. It accepts:
- JSON: an object keyed by `YYYY-MM-DD` with `{cost:number, tokens:number, models:string[]}`, or an array of such records each containing a `date`.
- CSV from Anthropic Console (recognizes `input tokens` + `output tokens`, `cost`, `model`, `date`).
- CSV from any other tool: it matches a date column (`date`/`day`), a token column (`token`, or separate input/output token columns), an optional cost column (`cost`/`price`), and an optional `model` column.
- Minimal 3-column CSV: `date,tokens,cost`.

## Auto-import (Claude Code)
The repo bundles `export-usage.js`, a zero-dependency Node script that reads Claude Code's local session logs (`~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl`), aggregates daily tokens (input + output + cache) and estimates cost from public list prices, then prints JSON ready to paste into the import box. Usage: `node export-usage.js --copy` (clipboard), `--out usage.json`, or `--days N`. It only reads local files; nothing is uploaded.

## Links
- Live demo: https://yifanliu734-coder.github.io/token-calendar/
- Source: https://github.com/yifanliu734-coder/token-calendar
- License: MIT

## Use / remix
MIT licensed — free to use, copy, modify, self-host, or fork. Because it is one HTML file with no dependencies, it is trivial to download, read end-to-end, embed, or adapt to a new usage format by extending the CSV/JSON parser.
